communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request: WA LexisNexis Master Contract - Participating Agencies
communications.communication:
DES PRR 26-0368,

This is an acknowledgment of your public records request below. On this website Contract Summary<https://apps.des.wa.gov/DESContracts/Home/ContractSummary/00220> you will find pricing exhibits, and contracts/amendments for Lexis Nexis. You can find this information as you scroll down under “Awarded Vendors” or a little bit further down there is a drop-down menu titled “Current Documents” that has these records as well. And on this website Statewide Contract (Master Contract) Sales Data by Customer, Contract, Vendor | Data.WA | State of Washington<https://data.wa.gov/Procurements-and-Contracts/Statewide-Contract-Master-Contract-Sales-Data-by-C/n8q6-4twj/data_preview>, you can find purchase and sales data. In the top right corner, there is a search bar where you can search for “00220” or “Employment income” and see the sales reported data and who purchased for this contract. There is a screen shot below showing this search as an example. It is important to use the “” symbols when typing in the contract number in the search to get an accurate search.

DES would not have purchase orders, participating addenda, or order forms as those would come from the departments that have used the contract themselves.

With this response your request is now closed, and this is our final response. DES does not intend to further address your public records request. The PRA’s one year statue of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run as of date of this communication. DES encourages you to ask any follow-up questions regarding our response. DES encourages you to notify DES of any concerns you may have about the adequacy of our response, compliance with the PRA or general concerns about any records we have provided. Please ask any question on or before May 26, 2026.

title: Port of Seattle Police Department Dive Unit Records (2010-2011) / Reference: Detective Steven Ivey Underwater Ordnance Recovery

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Port of Seattle Police Department Dive Unit Records (2010-2011) / Reference: Detective Steven Ivey Underwater Ordnance Recovery
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to and copies of public records regarding the Port of Seattle Police Department (POSPD) Dive Team's discovery, location, and recovery operations of "live" and "spent" World War II and Vietnam War era munitions/shells at the cruise ship terminals (Terminal 91 / Smith Cove).

To assist in locating these records, please note that these operations occurred primarily between April 2010 and December 2011, and heavily involved Detective Steven Ivey of the POSPD Dive Team (as publicly cited in his 2012 Officer of the Year commendation).

For context:
https://maritime-executive.com/article/video-additional-munitions-discovered-under-seattle-cruise-ship-terminal

A publicly accessible document details Officer Ivey's involvement:
https://scctv-portals-pos.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/attachments/2012/1298/RM_20120626_4b.pdf

Specifically, I am requesting the following items within the timeframe of April 1, 2010, through December 31, 2011:

Video Footage: Any and all video recordings, including but not limited to underwater SCUBA dive video footage, above-water operational or briefing videos, and media/surveillance or drone footage captures related to these specific underwater ordnance sweeps and multi-agency EOD recovery actions.

Operational & Investigative Reports: Any dive logs, incident reports, after-action reports, or tactical summaries detailing Detective Steven Ivey’s (and the broader dive team's) multi-hour underwater recovery efforts at the cruise terminals.

Photographs: Any underwater or topside photographs taken by POSPD personnel documenting the discovered munitions, the dive operations, or the handoff to military EOD units.

If any portion of these records is deemed exempt from disclosure, please redact the exempt portions and provide the remaining non-exempt records, along with an itemized exemption log citing the specific statutory exemption under RCW 42.56.

Thank you for your time and assistance with this request.

Suggested Search & Indexing Terms:
To assist with internal database, email, and archiving searches, the Port of Seattle may have cataloged these records under any of the following terms, which are included in the scope of this request:

Personnel/Units: "Steven Ivey", "Det. Ivey", "Detective Ivey", "POSPD Dive", "Dive Team", "Explosive Ordnance Disposal", "EOD".

Locations: "Terminal 91", "T-91", "Pier 90", "Pier 91", "Smith Cove", "Magnolia Bridge".

Items/Operations: "Munitions", "Ordnance", "WWII shells", "Vietnam shells", "Underwater sweep", "DMM", "UXO" (Unexploded Ordnance), "Officer of the Year".

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: FOIA Request: Policy 430 - Automated License Plate Readers (ALPR)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request: Policy 430 - Automated License Plate Readers (ALPR)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

According to Forest Park Police Department Policy 430, with reference to 625 ILCS 5/2-130, it is stated

- Section III / F: "The Chief of Police or their designee, serves as the ALPR Administrator for the agency"
- Section IV Procedures:
A: "No member of this department shall operate ALPR equipment or access ALPR data without first completing department-approved training"
D: "ALPR Administrator shall monitor to ensure the security of the information and compliance with applicable privacy laws"
G: "If an ALPR is used...a Street Camera / LPR Use Form must be completed and forwarded to the Patrol Lieutenant who will keep a record of all uses"
- Section I Investigative Procedures
Q1: "ALPR system audits should be conducted on a regular basis"
Q2: "The Chief or their designee should download and save monthly ALPR system User and Access data for at least two years and one month"

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records

The documents I am seeking include, but are not limited to:
1) Any document recording the name of the Forest Park ALPR Administrator, if this role is not fulfilled by the Chief of Police.

2) Training Materials: The department-approved training materials used as a prerequisite for sworn officers to use or access ALPR data as per section IV - A

3) Training Records: Certificates of completion or other records showing sworn officer completion of the training provided to staff as per section IV - A

4) Policies, procedures and records of how the ALPR Administrator monitors to ensure the security of the ALPR information to ensure compliance with applicable privacy laws as per section IV - D

5) Copies of the Street Camera / LPR Use Forms from September 2025 to present as per section IV - G

6) Policies, procedures and records of the ALPR system audits conducted per section Q of Policy 430

7) Monthly ALPR system User and Access data for the last two years and one month as per section Q


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

ll

communications.subject: 2026-IGFP-00761 McCarthy, Rebecca Final Response
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. McCarthy:

This responds to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to the U.S. Postal Service Office of Inspector General (OIG) for records related to:

  *   All records related to the engagement of Alvarez & Marsal by the United States Postal Service from January 1, 2026, to the present, including but not limited to:

  1.  The executed contract, statement of work, and any task orders or modifications
  2.  The procurement file, including any sole-source justification or competitive solicitation documents
  3.  Communications between USPS leadership (Postmaster General, Deputy Postmaster General, Chief Strategy Officer, Chief Financial Officer, General Counsel) and Alvarez & Marsal personnel concerning the scope of the engagement
  4.  Any interim or final deliverables, reports, presentations, or memoranda produced by Alvarez & Marsal for USPS
  5.  All invoices submitted and payments made.

FOIA is about records we have in our possession. The FOIA provides only for disclosing federal agency records and does not require government agencies to answer questions, conduct unreasonably burdensome research, or create records. The FOIA sheds light on an agency's performance of its statutory duties. The FOIA provides that any person has a right, enforceable in court, to obtain access to federal agency records, unless such records (or portions of them) are protected from public disclosure by one of nine exemptions or by one of three special law enforcement record exclusions.



The U.S. Postal Service comprises the Postal Service, the U.S. Postal Inspection Service (USPIS); the Postal Rate Commission (PRC); and the OIG; each with its own area of responsibility and individual FOIA office.

The Postal Service Records Office establishes procedures to ensure Postal Service records management practices comply with both the FOIA and Privacy Act. The Records Office is the designated custodian for non-law enforcement Postal Service records, including but not limited to contracting and employment/personnel records. Additional information regarding the Postal Service FOIA program can be found at Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) - Who We Are - About.usps.com<https://about.usps.com/who/legal/foia/?_gl=1*ls58y6*_ga*MzUzMDM5MzU1LjE3NTE4OTIwNzE.*_ga_QM3XHZ2B95*czE3NzA5MDY3OTckbzckZzAkdDE3NzA5MDY3OTgkajU5JGwwJGgw>.


The USPIS is the designated custodian for postal records relating to Postal Service law enforcement issues. The USPIS is a federal law enforcement entity charged with enforcing the Federal statutes involving mail fraud, illegal drugs, external mail theft, and other postal crimes, including revenue deficiencies, and are responsible for safety and security of all postal employees and facilities. Additional information regarding the USPIS FOIA program can be found at Terms of Service and Legal | USPIS<https://www.uspis.gov/terms-of-service#freedom-of-information-act>.



The OIG is an independent agency, created in 1997, with oversight authority over audits and investigations of Postal and Inspection Service fraud, abuse, and misconduct. The OIG's mission is to prevent, detect, and report fraud, theft, and misconduct, and promote efficiency in the operation of the Postal Service. Prior to this time, Postal Service audits and investigations were conducted by the USPIS.


While the OIG has "oversight authority" for activities of the above referenced entities, the OIG is a separate agency and does not have management authority over the daily operations of either the Postal Service or USPIS. In addition, the OIG FOIA office does not have access to records maintained by the Postal Service or UPSIS and cannot direct either FOIA office to release records under their control. Likewise, the OIG Deputy General Counsel has no authority to review FOIA determinations issued by the Postal Service or USPIS.


The OIG does not maintain the records you are seeking; therefore, we cannot assist you with this matter.



Because you are seeking records potentially maintained by the Postal Service, we are forwarding your request to their FOIA office for processing and direct response to you. If you have questions regarding this referral, please contact them online at https://pfoiapal.usps.com/, via email at foia12@usps.gov<mailto:foia12@usps.gov>, via phone at (202) 268-2608, via fax at (202) 268-5353, or in writing at:



Manager Records Office

U.S. Postal Service

475 L'Enfant Plaza SW, Room 1P830

Washington, DC  20260-1101

If you have questions regarding the processing of this request, please submit them in writing to foia@uspsoig.gov<mailto:foia@uspsoig.gov>. We will provide you an answer within two working days. You may also contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer. The contact information for OGIS is: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, Room 2510, 8601 Adelphi Road, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov<mailto:ogis@nara.gov>; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769.



As this completes our work on your request, we are closing your file here.

Sincerely,

USPS OIG FOIA Team

title: Non Profit Sales Tax Exemptions

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Non Profit Sales Tax Exemptions
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to Florida's Public Records Act (Chapter 119, Florida Statutes, 
also known as the Sunshine Law), I respectfully request the following 
public records from the Florida Department of Revenue:

A list of all nonprofit organizations currently holding a valid 
Consumer's Certificate of Exemption (Form DR-14) issued by the 
Department under Florida Statutes §212.08(7) and Rule 12A-1.038 F.A.C., 
as of the most recent date for which complete data is available.

For each organization, please include the following data fields where 
maintained by the Department:

  1. Organization legal name
  2. Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN / FEIN)
  3. Consumer's Certificate of Exemption number
  4. Mailing address (street, city, state, ZIP — minimum: city, state, ZIP)
  5. Exemption category (e.g., charitable, religious, educational, 
     scientific, parent-teacher organization, veterans' organization, 
     volunteer fire department, library, etc., per the categories listed 
     in §212.08(7))
  6. Date of original DR-14 issuance
  7. Current certificate effective date
  8. Current certificate expiration date (DR-14 certificates expire 
     after five years per the Department's renewal process)
  9. Current status (active, expired, revoked, suspended)
  10. NTEE or other organization-type classification code, if maintained

Please provide this information in CSV, Excel, or another machine-readable 
electronic format. If the source data is maintained in a database, an 
export from that database in its native format is strongly preferred 
over a re-keyed list or PDF report.

Basis for non-confidentiality at the individual-organization level:
The Department maintains multiple public-facing verification channels 
that allow any member of the public to confirm a specific organization's 
DR-14 exemption status:

  - The online Certificate Verification System at 
    floridarevenue.com/taxes/certificates
  - The FL Tax-Verify mobile application
  - The Department's automated nationwide toll-free telephone verification 
    system at 1(877)357-3725

These three publicly available verification mechanisms demonstrate that 
DR-14 status determinations are treated as non-confidential at the 
individual-organization level and are routinely disclosed by the 
Department on request. The records I am requesting are the same status 
determinations, provided in bulk format for efficiency.

To the extent the Department interprets F.S. §213.053 (taxpayer 
information confidentiality) to cover any of the requested fields, 
please note that §213.053 protects "information contained in returns, 
reports, accounts, or declarations received by the department," and 
that administrative status determinations issued by the Department 
under §212.08(7) are distinct from return information. The Department's 
practice of publicly verifying individual certificates further 
demonstrates that DR-14 status information falls outside §213.053's 
confidentiality scope. If the Department interprets §213.053 to cover 
any specific requested field, please identify the field(s) and provide 
the remaining fields separately rather than withholding the entire 
record. Per F.S. §119.07(1)(d), non-exempt portions of responsive 
records must be released even when some portions may be exempt.

I am requesting this information for research purposes 
relating to nonprofit-sector regulatory analysis. If any fee in excess 
of $50 is anticipated, please notify me before proceeding so that scope 
or format can be adjusted to reduce cost. Per F.S. §119.07(4)(d), I 
understand the Department may charge for the actual cost of duplication 
plus, where extensive use of agency resources is required, a reasonable 
service charge.

Per F.S. §119.07(1)(c), please acknowledge receipt of this request 
promptly and identify a reasonable time within which responsive records 
will be provided. If any portion of this request is denied, please 
provide the specific statutory basis for each denial under §119.07(1)(f).

Thank you for your assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

non-profit-researcher

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to New York's Freedom of Information Law (Public Officers Law 
Article 6, §§84-90), I respectfully request the following public records 
from the New York State Department of Taxation and Finance:

A list of all organizations currently holding a valid Form ST-119 Exempt 
Organization Certificate issued by the Department under Tax Law §1116 
and 20 NYCRR Part 529, as of the most recent date for which complete 
data is available.

For each organization, please include the following data fields where 
maintained by the Department:

  1. Organization legal name
  2. Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN / FEIN)
  3. Six-digit New York State sales tax exemption number
  4. Mailing address (street, city, state, ZIP — minimum: city, state, ZIP)
  5. Exempt organization category (charitable, educational, religious,
     scientific, etc.)
  6. Date of original ST-119 issuance
  7. Current status (active, expired, revoked, suspended)
  8. NTEE or other organization-type classification code, if maintained

Please provide this information in CSV, Excel, or another machine-readable 
electronic format. If the source data is maintained in a database, an 
export from that database in its native format is preferred over a 
re-keyed list or PDF report.

Basis for non-confidentiality at the individual level:
The Department's published guidance at tax.ny.gov/bus/st/exempt.htm 
instructs members of the public to contact the sales tax information 
center to verify any organization's exempt status. This practice 
demonstrates that ST-119 status determinations are treated as 
non-confidential at the individual-organization level and are 
routinely disclosed by the Department on request. The records I am 
requesting are the same status determinations, requested in bulk for 
efficiency.

Tax Law §697 confidentiality protections apply to tax returns and 
return information. Status determinations under §1116 are 
administrative determinations distinct from return information and are 
not within the §697 confidentiality scope. (See 20 NYCRR §2392.1 
distinguishing administrative records from return information.) If the 
Department interprets §697 to cover any of the requested fields, please 
identify the specific field(s) and provide the remaining fields 
separately rather than withholding the entire record. Per POL §89(4), 
a record is not exempt from disclosure simply because some portion of 
it might be exempt — non-exempt portions must be disclosed.

I am requesting this information for research purposes

Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

title: RFP List

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: RFP List
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all Requests for Proposals (RFPs) issued by the city with proposal openings between January 1, 2015 and the date this request is fulfilled, as tracked in any existing database, log, spreadsheet, procurement system, or other record the city already maintains for this purpose. To the extent the existing record(s) contain them, please include the following fields: RFP number, title, issuing agency or department, estimated or awarded contract value, proposal opening date, and awarded vendor. An export in the native electronic format (e.g., CSV, Excel, or database export) that the record is maintained would be preferred if possible.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: FW: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: RFP List
communications.communication:
Good afternoon:

This is in response to your email correspondence below. Please be advised that the City construes your request for records as a request coming under New Hampshire’s Right-to-Know law, RSA chapter 91-A.
Please be further advised that the City has no obligation to respond to requests from out-of-state corporations unless the corporation can show that it is located and/or has a nexus in New Hampshire. See McBurney v. Young, 569 U.S. 221 (2013). The City receives a large number of requests from out-of-state data collection corporations requesting financial and other reports to be submitted electronically to an email address and/or website. Due to the significant amount of time it takes for staff to research and generate reports in response to the out-of-state requests from data collection companies, the City will not transmit responses to such requests.

Please note that the City is required to make documents which are subject to disclosure, accessible for public review on its regular business premises (RSA Chapter 91-A:4,I). In accordance with RSA 91-A, the City would provide you, or a Representative (Mr. Van Fleet), the opportunity to personally review records, free of charge, at City Hall.  To the extent that your Representative would request copies of any documents in electronic format, your Representative may bring a sealed/unopened thumb drive and the City will upload the documents to the thumb drive, free of charge. If your Representative does not have a sealed/unopened thumb drive at the time of the scheduled visit to City Hall, we have new thumb drives that may be purchased at cost price.

Please contact our office at 603-225-8505 to schedule a date and time for you to review the documents at City Hall. The City will require approximately thirty (30) days prior notice of the date of when you intend to review the documents so that we can have the documents ready for your review upon your arrival.

Notwithstanding the aforementioned, please be advised that you may find information you are seeking on the City’s website at https://www.concordnh.gov/1092/Bids-Proposals-Quotations.

Thank you,

Velinda Diaz
